input.background: We calculate the $95 \%$ confidence interval for $\mu_1-\mu_2$ for the cholesterol illustrative data (see Table 12.5).
input.question: Derive degrees of freedom by the Welch method and calculate the $95 \%$ confidence interval.

output.answer: The value of $t_{8.61, .975}$ via a software utility is 2.306. The $95 \%$ confidence interval is $0.154$ to $1.400(\mathrm{mmol} / \mathrm{L})$.

mu_1.value: Mean cholesterol level for group 1 (specific values not provided in the problem)
mu_1.description: Mean cholesterol level for the first group in the illustrative data.

mu_2.value: Mean cholesterol level for group 2 (specific values not provided in the problem)
mu_2.description: Mean cholesterol level for the second group in the illustrative data.

s_1.value: Standard deviation of cholesterol levels for group 1 (specific values not provided in the problem)
s_1.description: Standard deviation of cholesterol levels for the first group in the illustrative data.

s_2.value: Standard deviation of cholesterol levels for group 2 (specific values not provided in the problem)
s_2.description: Standard deviation of cholesterol levels for the second group in the illustrative data.

n_1.value: Sample size for group 1 (specific values not provided in the problem)
n_1.description: Sample size for the first group in the illustrative data.

n_2.value: Sample size for group 2 (specific values not provided in the problem)
n_2.description: Sample size for the second group in the illustrative data.

degrees_of_freedom.value: Calculated using the Welch method (formula depends on s_1, s_2, n_1, n_2)
degrees_of_freedom.description: Degrees of freedom derived using the Welch method for comparing two groups.

confidence_interval.value: 95% confidence interval for mu_1 - mu_2 (calculated using degrees_of_freedom, s_1, s_2, n_1, n_2)
confidence_interval.description: The range within which the true difference in mean cholesterol levels between the two groups is expected to lie with 95% confidence.
